$comment: Shipped <sys/types.h> POSIX typedefs + the FIRST subdirectory descriptor (sys/types.json). off_t/ssize_t -> i64, mode_t -> u32; exit 42 witnesses the subdir resolution + typedef injection on the unix ABIs (elf x86-64 + arm64-qemu + macho). NO windows-pe arm: <sys/types.h> is a POSIX header now declared `availableObjectFormats:[elf,macho]`, so a pe compile FAILS LOUD (c8 per-target availability) — proven by the dedicated shipped_header_unavailable_pe error example. RED-on-disable: without the resolveSystemDescriptor subdir-preserving fix, <sys/types.h> maps to a non-existent types.json and the header fails to resolve -> compile fails. The release arm confirms the full shipped pipeline.
